434:         name: run.name,
435:         status: normalizeCheckRunStatus(run.status),
436:         conclusion: run.conclusion,
437:       }));
438: 
439:       const failing = items.filter(isCheckRunFailing).length;
440:       const pending = items.filter(isCheckRunPending).length;
441: 
442:       return {
443:         sourceState: items.length === 0 ? 'ok-empty' : 'ok',
444:         total: items.length,
445:         failing,
446:         pending,
447:         items,
448:       };
449:     } catch (error) {
450:       if (error instanceof GitHubApiError) {
451:         if (error.code === 'FORBIDDEN' || error.code === 'NOT_FOUND') {
452:           return { sourceState: 'unavailable', total: 0, failing: 0, pending: 0, items: [] };
453:         }
454:       }
455:       return { sourceState: 'error', total: 0, failing: 0, pending: 0, items: [] };
456:     }
457:   }
458: 
459:   async getCombinedStatus(owner: string, repo: string, ref: string): Promise<StatusSignals> {
460:     interface GitHubCombinedStatusResponse {
461:       state: string;
462:       statuses: Array<{
463:         context: string;
464:         state: string;
465:         description: string | null;
466:       }>;
467:     }
468: 
469:     try {
470:       const data = await this.get<GitHubCombinedStatusResponse>(
471:         `/repos/${owner}/${repo}/commits/${ref}/status`
472:       );
473: 
474:       const statuses: NormalizedCommitStatus[] = data.statuses.map((s) => ({
475:         context: s.context,
476:         state: normalizeCommitStatusState(s.state),
477:         description: s.description,
478:       }));
479: 
480:       return {
481:         sourceState: statuses.length === 0 ? 'ok-empty' : 'ok',
482:         state: statuses.length === 0 ? null : normalizeCommitStatusState(data.state),
483:         statuses,
484:       };
485:     } catch (error) {
486:       if (error instanceof GitHubApiError) {
487:         if (error.code === 'FORBIDDEN' || error.code === 'NOT_FOUND') {
488:           return { sourceState: 'unavailable', state: null, statuses: [] };
489:         }
490:       }
491:       return { sourceState: 'error', state: null, statuses: [] };
492:     }
493:   }
494: 
495:   async getCodeScanningAlerts(owner: string, repo: string, ref: string): Promise<ScanningSignals> {
496:     interface GitHubCodeScanningAlert {
497:       number: number;
498:       rule: {
499:         id: string | null;
500:         severity: string | null;
501:       };
502:       state: string;
503:       most_recent_instance: {
504:         location: {
505:           path: string;
506:           start_line: number;
507:         } | null;
508:       } | null;
509:     }
510: 
511:     try {
512:       const alerts = await this.get<GitHubCodeScanningAlert[]>(
513:         `/repos/${owner}/${repo}/code-scanning/alerts`,
514:         { params: { state: 'open', ref, per_page: 100 } }
515:       );
516: 
517:       const items: NormalizedScanningAlert[] = alerts.map((alert) => {
518:         const severity = normalizeCodeScanningSeverity(alert.rule.severity);
519:         const location = alert.most_recent_instance?.location
520:           ? `${alert.most_recent_instance.location.path}:${alert.most_recent_instance.location.start_line}`
521:           : 'unknown';
522:         return {
523:           number: alert.number,
524:           ruleId: alert.rule.id ?? 'unknown',
525:           severity,
526:           state: normalizeAlertState(alert.state),
527:           location,
528:         };
529:       });
530: 
531:       const highSeverityCount = items.filter(
532:         (a) => a.severity === 'critical' || a.severity === 'high'
533:       ).length;
534: 
535:       return {
536:         sourceState: items.length === 0 ? 'ok-empty' : 'ok',
537:         openAlerts: items.length,
538:         highSeverityCount,
539:         items,
540:       };
541:     } catch (error) {
542:       if (error instanceof GitHubApiError) {
543:         if (
544:           error.code === 'FORBIDDEN' ||
545:           error.code === 'NOT_FOUND' ||
546:           error.code === 'VALIDATION_ERROR'
547:         ) {
548:           return { sourceState: 'unavailable', openAlerts: 0, highSeverityCount: 0, items: [] };
549:         }
550:       }
551:       return { sourceState: 'error', openAlerts: 0, highSeverityCount: 0, items: [] };
552:     }
553:   }
554: 
555:   async validateToken(): Promise<boolean> {
556:     try {
557:       await this.client.get('/user');
558:       return true;
559:     } catch {
560:       return false;
561:     }
562:   }
563: }
564: 
565: export function createGitHubService(config: AppConfig): GitHubService {
566:   return new GitHubService(config);
567: }

26: // Helper to read the private axios baseURL

28:   // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
29:   return (service as any).client.defaults.baseURL as string;
30: }

36: describe('GitHubService — URL routing', () => {

38:     beforeEach(() => {

45:       expect(getBaseURL(svc)).toBe('/api/github');

220:     );
221:   });
222: });
223: 
224: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
225: // Error parsing tests
226: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
227: 
228: function makeAxiosError(
229:   status: number,
230:   data: Record<string, unknown>,
231:   headers: Record<string, string> = {}
232: ): AxiosError {
233:   return new AxiosError('Request failed', 'ERR_BAD_REQUEST', undefined, undefined, {
234:     data,
235:     status,
236:     statusText: String(status),
237:     headers: AxiosHeaders.from(headers),
238:     config: {
239:       headers: AxiosHeaders.from({}),
240:     },
241:   });
242: }
243: 
244: describe('parseGitHubError', () => {
245:   it('maps authentication failures to a typed auth error', () => {
246:     const error = makeAxiosError(401, { message: 'Bad credentials' });
247:     const parsed = parseGitHubError(error);
248: 
249:     expect(parsed).toBeInstanceOf(GitHubApiError);
250:     expect(parsed.code).toBe('AUTHENTICATION_ERROR');
251:     expect(parsed.status).toBe(401);
252:     expect(parsed.userMessage).toContain('Personal Access Token');
253:   });
254: 
255:   it('maps rate limiting failures and extracts reset metadata', () => {
256:     const error = makeAxiosError(
257:       403,
258:       { message: 'API rate limit exceeded' },
259:       {
260:         'x-ratelimit-remaining': '0',
261:         'x-ratelimit-reset': '1735689600',
262:         'retry-after': '60',
263:       }
264:     );
265: 
266:     const parsed = parseGitHubError(error);
267: 
268:     expect(parsed.code).toBe('RATE_LIMITED');
269:     expect(parsed.retryAfterSeconds).toBe(60);
270:     expect(parsed.rateLimitResetAt).toBe('2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z');
271:   });
272: 
273:   it('maps network errors when no response is present', () => {
274:     const error = new AxiosError('Network Error', 'ERR_NETWORK');
275:     const parsed = parseGitHubError(error);
276: 
277:     expect(parsed.code).toBe('NETWORK_ERROR');
278:     expect(parsed.status).toBeNull();
279:     expect(parsed.userMessage).toContain('Unable to reach GitHub');
280:   });
281: });

2:  * Shared normalized signal snapshot builder.
3:  * Consumed by both summary and chat prompt construction in llm.ts.
4:  *
5:  * Source-state semantics are explicitly preserved:
6:  *  - 'ok'        → signals fetched and have items
7:  *  - 'ok-empty'  → signals fetched, no items (not failing, not unavailable)
8:  *  - 'unavailable' → permission / feature limit — must NOT be treated as passing
9:  *  - 'error'     → fetch failed — must NOT be treated as passing
10:  */
11: 
12: import type {
13:   NormalizedCheckRun,
14:   NormalizedScanningAlert,
15:   PRSignals,
16: } from '../types';
17: 
18: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
19: // Caps
20: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
21: 
22: export const SUMMARY_SIGNAL_CAPS = {
23:   failingChecks: 5,
24:   pendingChecks: 3,
25:   failingStatuses: 5,
26:   pendingStatuses: 3,
27:   scanningAlerts: 5,
28: } as const;
29: 
30: export const CHAT_SIGNAL_CAPS = {
31:   failingChecks: 3,
32:   pendingChecks: 2,
33:   failingStatuses: 3,
34:   pendingStatuses: 2,
35:   scanningAlerts: 3,
36: } as const;
37: 
38: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
39: // Deterministic ranking helpers (task 2.5)
40: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41: 
42: const FAILING_CONCLUSIONS = new Set(['action_required', 'failure', 'timed_out', 'cancelled']);
43: 
44: const CONCLUSION_RANK: Record<string, number> = {
45:   action_required: 0,
46:   failure: 1,
47:   timed_out: 2,
48:   cancelled: 3,
49: };
50: 
51: const SEVERITY_RANK: Record<string, number> = {
52:   critical: 0,
53:   high: 1,
54:   medium: 2,
55:   low: 3,
56:   error: 4,
57:   warning: 5,
58:   note: 6,
59:   none: 7,
60:   unknown: 8,
61: };
62: 
63: export function rankAndCapFailingChecks(
64:   items: NormalizedCheckRun[],
65:   limit: number
66: ): NormalizedCheckRun[] {
67:   return items
68:     .filter(
69:       (r) => r.status === 'completed' && FAILING_CONCLUSIONS.has(r.conclusion ?? '')
70:     )
71:     .sort(
72:       (a, b) =>
73:         (CONCLUSION_RANK[a.conclusion ?? ''] ?? 99) -
74:         (CONCLUSION_RANK[b.conclusion ?? ''] ?? 99)
75:     )
76:     .slice(0, limit);
77: }
78: 
79: export function rankAndCapPendingChecks(
80:   items: NormalizedCheckRun[],
81:   limit: number
82: ): NormalizedCheckRun[] {
83:   return items.filter((r) => r.status !== 'completed').slice(0, limit);
84: }
85: 
86: export function rankAndCapScanningAlerts(
87:   items: NormalizedScanningAlert[],
88:   limit: number
89: ): NormalizedScanningAlert[] {
90:   return [...items]
91:     .sort(
92:       (a, b) => (SEVERITY_RANK[a.severity] ?? 99) - (SEVERITY_RANK[b.severity] ?? 99)
93:     )
94:     .slice(0, limit);
95: }
96: 
97: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
98: // Prompt snapshot builders (task 2.3 / 2.6)
99: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
100: 
101: /**
102:  * Richer signal snapshot for summary generation context.
103:  * Includes up to SUMMARY_SIGNAL_CAPS items per category with named details.
104:  */
105: export function buildSignalSnapshotForSummary(signals: PRSignals): string {
106:   const lines: string[] = ['### CI/Check and Scanning Signals'];
107: 
108:   // --- Checks ---
109:   const { checks } = signals;
110:   if (checks.sourceState === 'unavailable') {
111:     lines.push(
112:       '- Check runs: unavailable (permission or feature configuration limit — not confirmed passing)'
113:     );
114:   } else if (checks.sourceState === 'error') {
115:     lines.push('- Check runs: error (could not fetch — treat as unknown, not passing)');
116:   } else if (checks.sourceState === 'ok-empty') {
117:     lines.push('- Check runs: none configured');
118:   } else {
119:     const topFailing = rankAndCapFailingChecks(checks.items, SUMMARY_SIGNAL_CAPS.failingChecks);
120:     const topPending = rankAndCapPendingChecks(checks.items, SUMMARY_SIGNAL_CAPS.pendingChecks);
121:     const statusParts: string[] = [];
122:     if (checks.failing > 0) statusParts.push(`${checks.failing} failing`);
123:     if (checks.pending > 0) statusParts.push(`${checks.pending} pending`);
124:     if (statusParts.length === 0) statusParts.push('all passing');
125: 
126:     lines.push(`- Check runs: ${statusParts.join(', ')} (${checks.total} total)`);
127:     if (topFailing.length > 0) {
128:       lines.push(
129:         `  Failing: ${topFailing
130:           .map((r) => `"${r.name}" (${r.conclusion ?? 'unknown'})`)
131:           .join(', ')}`
132:       );
133:     }
134:     if (topPending.length > 0) {
135:       lines.push(`  Pending: ${topPending.map((r) => r.name).join(', ')}`);
136:     }
137:   }
138: 
139:   // --- Commit statuses ---
140:   const { statuses } = signals;
141:   if (statuses.sourceState === 'unavailable') {
142:     lines.push(
143:       '- Commit statuses: unavailable (permission limit — not confirmed passing)'
144:     );
145:   } else if (statuses.sourceState === 'error') {
146:     lines.push('- Commit statuses: error (could not fetch — treat as unknown, not passing)');
147:   } else if (statuses.sourceState === 'ok-empty') {
148:     lines.push('- Commit statuses: none');
149:   } else {
150:     const failingStatuses = statuses.statuses.filter(
151:       (s) => s.state === 'failure' || s.state === 'error'
152:     );
153:     const pendingStatuses = statuses.statuses.filter((s) => s.state === 'pending');
154: 
155:     lines.push(`- Commit statuses: ${statuses.state ?? 'unknown'}`);
156:     if (failingStatuses.length > 0) {
157:       lines.push(
158:         `  Failing: ${failingStatuses
159:           .slice(0, SUMMARY_SIGNAL_CAPS.failingStatuses)
160:           .map((s) => `"${s.context}"`)
161:           .join(', ')}`
162:       );
163:     }
164:     if (pendingStatuses.length > 0) {
165:       lines.push(
166:         `  Pending: ${pendingStatuses
167:           .slice(0, SUMMARY_SIGNAL_CAPS.pendingStatuses)
168:           .map((s) => `"${s.context}"`)
169:           .join(', ')}`
170:       );
171:     }
172:   }
173: 
174:   // --- Code scanning ---
175:   const { scanning } = signals;
176:   if (scanning.sourceState === 'unavailable') {
177:     lines.push(
178:       '- Code scanning: unavailable (permission or feature configuration limit — not confirmed passing)'
179:     );
180:   } else if (scanning.sourceState === 'error') {
181:     lines.push('- Code scanning: error (could not fetch — treat as unknown, not passing)');
182:   } else if (scanning.sourceState === 'ok-empty') {
183:     lines.push('- Code scanning: no open alerts');
184:   } else {
185:     const topAlerts = rankAndCapScanningAlerts(
186:       scanning.items,
187:       SUMMARY_SIGNAL_CAPS.scanningAlerts
188:     );
189:     lines.push(
190:       `- Code scanning: ${scanning.openAlerts} open alert(s) (${scanning.highSeverityCount} critical/high severity)`
191:     );
192:     if (topAlerts.length > 0) {
193:       lines.push(
194:         `  Top alerts: ${topAlerts
195:           .map((a) => `"${a.ruleId}" @ ${a.location} (${a.severity})`)
196:           .join(', ')}`
197:       );
198:     }
199:   }
200: 
201:   return lines.join('\n');
202: }
203: 
204: /**
205:  * Compact signal snapshot for chat system context.
206:  * Prioritizes brevity to preserve diff/review context budget.
207:  */
208: export function buildSignalSnapshotForChat(signals: PRSignals): string {
209:   const parts: string[] = [];
210: 
211:   // --- Checks ---
212:   const { checks } = signals;
213:   if (checks.sourceState === 'unavailable') {
214:     parts.push('Checks: unavailable (not confirmed passing)');
215:   } else if (checks.sourceState === 'error') {
216:     parts.push('Checks: error (not confirmed passing)');
217:   } else if (checks.sourceState === 'ok') {
218:     if (checks.failing > 0 || checks.pending > 0) {
219:       const topFailing = rankAndCapFailingChecks(checks.items, CHAT_SIGNAL_CAPS.failingChecks);
220:       const topPending = rankAndCapPendingChecks(checks.items, CHAT_SIGNAL_CAPS.pendingChecks);
221:       const statusParts: string[] = [];
222:       if (checks.failing > 0) statusParts.push(`${checks.failing} failing`);
223:       if (checks.pending > 0) statusParts.push(`${checks.pending} pending`);
224:       const names = [
225:         ...topFailing.map((r) => r.name),
226:         ...topPending.map((r) => r.name),
227:       ].slice(0, 5);
228:       parts.push(`Checks: ${statusParts.join(', ')} [${names.join(', ')}]`);
229:     } else {
230:       parts.push('Checks: all passing');
231:     }
232:     // ok-empty: omit for compactness
233:   }
234: 
235:   // --- Statuses ---
236:   const { statuses } = signals;
237:   if (statuses.sourceState === 'unavailable') {
238:     parts.push('Statuses: unavailable (not confirmed passing)');
239:   } else if (statuses.sourceState === 'error') {
240:     parts.push('Statuses: error (not confirmed passing)');
241:   } else if (statuses.sourceState === 'ok' && statuses.state && statuses.state !== 'success') {
242:     const failingStatuses = statuses.statuses
243:       .filter((s) => s.state === 'failure' || s.state === 'error')
244:       .slice(0, CHAT_SIGNAL_CAPS.failingStatuses)
245:       .map((s) => s.context);
246:     const detail = failingStatuses.length > 0 ? ` [${failingStatuses.join(', ')}]` : '';
247:     parts.push(`Statuses: ${statuses.state}${detail}`);
248:   }
249: 
250:   // --- Scanning ---
251:   const { scanning } = signals;
252:   if (scanning.sourceState === 'unavailable') {
253:     parts.push('Scanning: unavailable (not confirmed passing)');
254:   } else if (scanning.sourceState === 'error') {
255:     parts.push('Scanning: error (not confirmed passing)');
256:   } else if (scanning.sourceState === 'ok' && scanning.openAlerts > 0) {
257:     const topAlerts = rankAndCapScanningAlerts(
258:       scanning.items,
259:       CHAT_SIGNAL_CAPS.scanningAlerts
260:     );
261:     parts.push(
262:       `Scanning: ${scanning.openAlerts} open (${scanning.highSeverityCount} high+) [${topAlerts.map((a) => a.ruleId).join(', ')}]`
263:     );
264:   }
265: 
266:   if (parts.length === 0) return '';
267:   return `CI/Scanning: ${parts.join(' | ')}`;
268: }

</content>

1: # PR Review Chatbot - Implementation Plan
2: 
3: ## Priority: UI and LLM Integration First
4: **Key Change**: Use dummy data for PR content to allow immediate interaction with the LLM without needing GitHub API credentials. GitHub integration can be added later.
5: 
6: ---
7: 
8: ## Tech Stack
9: - Vite + React 18 + TypeScript
10: - Redux Toolkit for state management
11: - Axios for HTTP
12: - Tailwind CSS for styling
13: - Radix UI for accessible components
14: 
15: ---
16: 
17: ## Implementation Phases
18: 
19: ### ✅ Phase 1: Project Setup (COMPLETED)
20: - [x] Initialize Vite + React + TypeScript project
21: - [x] Install dependencies (Redux Toolkit, Axios, Tailwind, Radix UI, react-markdown, react-syntax-highlighter, lucide-react)

102: ```

104: VITE_LLM_API_KEY=         # LLM API Key
105: VITE_GITHUB_INSTANCE=     # "github.com" or URL for GHES
106: VITE_LLM_BACKEND=         # "openai" or "litellm"

108: VITE_LLM_MODEL=           # Model name

110: ```

112: ### Runtime Override

117: ## Architecture

121: src/services/
122: ├── github.ts      # Fetch PRs, details, diffs, comments (supports GHES)

124: └── dummyData.ts   # Sample PR data for demo mode

128: ```
129: src/store/
130: ├── index.ts              # Store configuration
131: ├── hooks.ts              # Typed useAppDispatch / useAppSelector
132: └── slices/
133:     ├── configSlice.ts    # App config (env defaults + runtime overrides)
134:     ├── prsSlice.ts       # Current PR data
135:     └── chatSlice.ts      # Messages + streaming state
136: ```
137: 
138: ### Components
139: ```
140: src/components/
141: ├── SettingsDialog.tsx   # Runtime config override
142: ├── Sidebar.tsx          # Repo/PR navigation (or demo mode toggle)
143: ├── ChatWindow.tsx       # Chat messages + input
144: └── PRViewer.tsx         # File diffs, metadata
145: ```
146: 
147: ---
148: 
149: ## Data Types
150: 
151: ### GitHub PR
152: ```typescript
153: interface PullRequest {
154:   id: number;
155:   number: number;
156:   title: string;
157:   body: string | null;
158:   state: 'open' | 'closed';
159:   merged: boolean;
160:   merged_at: string | null;
161:   user: GitHubUser;
162:   created_at: string;
163:   updated_at: string;
164:   head: { ref: string; sha: string; repo: { full_name: string } | null };
165:   base: { ref: string; sha: string; repo: { full_name: string } };
166:   url: string;
167:   html_url: string;
168:   diff_url: string;
169:   additions: number;
170:   deletions: number;
171:   changed_files: number;
172:   comments: number;
173:   review_comments: number;
174:   commits: number;
175:   labels: Array<{ id: number; name: string; color: string }>;
176:   requested_reviewers: GitHubUser[];
177: }
178: ```
179: 
180: ### Chat Message
181: ```typescript
182: interface ChatMessage {
183:   id: string;
184:   role: 'user' | 'assistant' | 'system';
185:   content: string;
186:   timestamp: number;
187:   isStreaming?: boolean;
188:   error?: string;
189: }
190: ```
191: 
192: ### App Config
193: ```typescript
194: interface AppConfig {
195:   githubPat: string;
196:   llmApiKey: string;
197:   githubInstance: string;
198:   llmBackend: 'openai' | 'litellm';
199:   llmEndpoint: string;
200:   llmModel: string;
201:   demoMode: boolean;
202: }
203: ```
204: 
205: ---
206: 
207: ## Error Handling
208: 
209: ### LLM API Errors
210: - Invalid API key: Error shown in message bubble
211: - Rate limiting: Error shown in message bubble
212: - Network errors: Error shown in message bubble
213: - Streaming errors: Graceful fallback with error message
214: 
215: ### GitHub API Errors (when enabled)
216: - Invalid PAT: Prompt to update token
217: - Rate limiting: Show remaining requests
218: - Not found (404): Repository/PR not found
219: 
220: ---
221: 
222: ### ⏳ Phase 8: Code Quality & UX Improvements (PENDING)
223: 
224: Identified during initial code review. These are non-blocking improvements to address after the Phase 7 polish work.
225: 
226: #### Quality
227: - [x] **ESLint v9 flat config** (`eslint.config.js`) — Added repository-level flat config so `npm run lint` works with ESLint 9.
228: - [x] **`getPRFiles` pagination** (`src/services/github.ts:96`) — The GitHub API caps file lists at 100 per page. PRs with >100 changed files silently return incomplete data. Add pagination (loop until `response.data.length < perPage`) or at minimum show a warning banner in `PRViewer` when the file count equals the page limit.
229: - [x] **Stable React keys in `DiffView`** (`src/components/PRViewer.tsx:41`) — Diff lines currently use array index as `key`. Replace with a content-based key (e.g. `` `${i}-${line.slice(0, 8)}` ``) to prevent React reusing DOM nodes incorrectly when switching between files.
230: 
231: #### UX
232: - [x] **Persist dark mode preference** (`src/App.tsx:10`) — Dark mode state resets on every page reload. Initialise from `localStorage` and respect the OS `prefers-color-scheme` media query:
233:   ```ts
234:   const [darkMode, setDarkMode] = useState(
235:     () => localStorage.getItem('darkMode') === 'true'
236:       || window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches
237:   );
238:   ```
239:   Also persist the toggle: `localStorage.setItem('darkMode', String(!darkMode))` inside `toggleDarkMode`.
240: - [x] **Wire up the Refresh button** (`src/components/Sidebar.tsx:111`) — The refresh `<button>` in the sidebar is rendered in GitHub mode but has no `onClick` handler. Either connect it to a `fetchPullRequests` thunk (part of Phase 7 GitHub UI work) or hide it with `{false && ...}` until that feature is ready, to avoid confusing users with a dead control.
241: 
242: ---
243: 
244: ## OpenSpec Alignment Notes (2026-03-14)
245: 
246: - `openspec/changes/add-pr-summary-tab/tasks.md` was updated to capture quick-orientation behavior decisions from explore mode.
247: - Summary behavior target: 2-4 line orientation section, adaptive `Focus Areas` with bounds `0..4`, and valid success output when focus areas are omitted for simple PRs.
248: - Synced delta spec `openspec/changes/add-pr-summary-tab/specs/pr-review-summary/spec.md` into new main spec `openspec/specs/pr-review-summary/spec.md`.
249: - Added change scaffold `openspec/changes/update-pr-review-summary-ui-readability/` with completed proposal, design, spec delta, and tasks artifacts for summary UI readability improvements.
250: - Refined `update-pr-review-summary-ui-readability` scope to panelized Summary display: Orientation and each Focus Area render as separate cards styled like Comments/Reviews, with blue/yellow title accents and no spec contract changes.
251: 
252: ---
253: 
254: ## ✅ Phase 9: PR Summary Tab (COMPLETED)
255: 
256: - [x] Extend `AppConfig` with summary fields: `summaryEnabled`, `summaryPrompt`, `summaryCommands`
257: - [x] Add/export `DEFAULT_SUMMARY_PROMPT` and keep reset behavior prompt-only
258: - [x] Add PR commits API loading (`getPRCommits`) and Redux state wiring
259: - [x] Add summary lifecycle state in `prsSlice` (`status`, `content`, `generatedAt`, `error`)
260: - [x] Implement summary orchestration utilities:
261:   - per-PR+head+prompt cache keying
262:   - sessionStorage read/write cache
263:   - one-per-minute per-PR-head rate limiting
264:   - empty textual diff detection
265: - [x] Extend LLM service with structured summary prompt assembly (orientation + adaptive focus areas)
266: - [x] Add Summary tab UI in `PRViewer` with loading/success/empty/error states and timestamp footer
267: - [x] Wire summary generation on PR selection/load and keep summary separate from chat history/context
268: - [x] Add/expand tests for config, GitHub service, PR slice, summary utilities, LLM prompt, Settings UI, and Summary tab rendering
269: - [x] Update README and agent guidance for summary behavior, fallback states, and output contract
270: 
271: ---
272: 
273: ## OpenSpec Implementation Updates (2026-03-14)
274: 
275: - [x] Completed OpenSpec change `update-pr-review-summary-ui-readability` implementation tasks (8/8) for panelized Summary tab readability updates.
276: - [x] Updated Summary success rendering in `src/components/PRViewer.tsx` to show an `Orientation` panel and per-item `Focus Area N` panels with blue/yellow title accents.
277: - [x] Kept summary generation flow and fallback state semantics unchanged (loading/empty/error behavior preserved).
278: - [x] Updated `src/components/PRViewer.summary.test.tsx` assertions for orientation-only and orientation+focus-area panelized rendering.
279: - [x] Validation run completed with `npm run lint && npm run build && npm run test`.
280: 
281: ## OpenSpec Explore Decision Updates (2026-03-16)
282: 
283: - [x] Clarified `repository-watcher` UX scope: `repo-pr-list` renders in the left `PR Review` pane (not center chat area or right PR viewer pane).
284: - [x] Captured sidebar navigator behavior: controls/watchlist view and repo list view with explicit Back action.
285: - [x] Captured `Load PR` behavior parity with demo mode: when PR number is provided, show a list containing one selected PR.
286: - [x] Updated change artifacts (`proposal.md`, `design.md`, `tasks.md`, `specs/repo-pr-list/spec.md`, `specs/watched-repos/spec.md`) to reflect the decision.
287: 
288: ## OpenSpec Implementation Updates (2026-03-16)

291: - [x] Added watched repository state and persistence via `src/store/slices/watchedReposSlice.ts`.
292: - [x] Added repository PR list loading in `src/store/slices/prsSlice.ts` using existing `listPullRequests()` integration.
293: - [x] Added sidebar repo-list UI mode with Back navigation and new `src/components/PRList.tsx` component.
294: - [x] Updated sidebar to support optional PR number flow (`Load PR` vs `Load All PRs`) and watchlist interactions.
295: - [x] Added tests for watched repos, repository PR list loading, and sidebar list selection flows.
296: - [x] Validation run completed with `npm run lint && npm run build && npm run test`.

metadata.preview:
# PR Review Chatbot - Implementation Plan

## Priority: UI and LLM Integration First
**Key Change**: Use dummy data for PR content to allow immediate interaction with the LLM without needing GitHub API credentials. GitHub integration can be added later.

---

## Tech Stack
- Vite + React 18 + TypeScript
- Redux Toolkit for state management
- Axios for HTTP
- Tailwind CSS for styling
- Radix UI for accessible components

---

## Implementation Phases

### ✅ Phase 1: Project Setup (COMPLETED)
- [x] Initialize Vite + React + TypeScript project

Index: /Users/mark/sandbox/personal/pr-review-chatbot/src/components/PRViewer.signals.test.tsx
===================================================================
--- /Users/mark/sandbox/personal/pr-review-chatbot/src/components/PRViewer.signals.test.tsx
+++ /Users/mark/sandbox/personal/pr-review-chatbot/src/components/PRViewer.signals.test.tsx
@@ -0,0 +1,227 @@
+import { configureStore } from '@reduxjs/toolkit';
+import { Provider } from 'react-redux';
+import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
+import { render, screen, within } from '@testing-library/react';
+import userEvent from '@testing-library/user-event';
+import { PRViewer } from './PRViewer';
+import configReducer from '../store/slices/configSlice';
+import prsReducer from '../store/slices/prsSlice';
+import chatReducer from '../store/slices/chatSlice';
+import type { PullRequest } from '../types';
+
+const selectedPRFixture: PullRequest = {
+  id: 1,
+  number: 42,
+  title: 'Signals rendering fixture',
+  body: 'Fixture body',
+  state: 'open',
+  merged: false,
+  merged_at: null,
+  user: { login: 'alice', avatar_url: '', html_url: '' },
+  created_at: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z',
+  updated_at: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z',
+  head: { ref: 'feature', sha: 'abcdef123456', repo: { full_name: 'org/repo' } },
+  base: { ref: 'main', sha: 'def', repo: { full_name: 'org/repo' } },
+  url: '',
+  html_url: '',
+  diff_url: '',
+  additions: 10,
+  deletions: 2,
+  changed_files: 2,
+  comments: 0,
+  review_comments: 0,
+  commits: 2,
+  labels: [],
+  requested_reviewers: [],
+};
+
+function renderViewer(signalsState: {
+  status: 'idle' | 'loading' | 'success' | 'error';
+  data: unknown;
+  error: string | null;
+}) {
+  const store = configureStore({
+    reducer: {
+      config: configReducer,
+      prs: prsReducer,
+      chat: chatReducer,
+    },
+    preloadedState: {
+      config: {
+        config: {
+          githubPat: '',
+          llmApiKey: '',
+          githubInstance: 'github.com',
+          llmBackend: 'openai' as const,
+          llmEndpoint: 'https://api.openai.com/v1',
+          llmModel: 'gpt-4o',
+          demoMode: false,
+          summaryEnabled: true,
+          summaryPrompt: 'default summary prompt',
+          summaryCommands: '',
+        },
+      },
+      prs: {
+        selectedPR: selectedPRFixture,
+        activeRepository: null,
+        prList: [],
+        files: [],
+        comments: [],
+        reviewComments: [],
+        reviews: [],
+        commits: [],
+        summary: {
+          status: 'idle' as const,
+          content: null,
+          generatedAt: null,
+          error: null,
+          requestKey: null,
+          citations: [],
+          hasUncitedContent: false,
+        },
+        signals: {
+          ...signalsState,
+          requestKey: null,
+        },
+        isLoading: false,
+        error: null,
+        loadingByResource: {
+          metadata: false,
+          files: false,
+          comments: false,
+          reviewComments: false,
+          reviews: false,
+          commits: false,
+          prList: false,
+        },
+        errorByResource: {
+          metadata: null,
+          files: null,
+          comments: null,
+          reviewComments: null,
+          reviews: null,
+          commits: null,
+          prList: null,
+        },
+        latestRequestKeyByResource: {
+          metadata: null,
+          files: null,
+          comments: null,
+          reviewComments: null,
+          reviews: null,
+          commits: null,
+        },
+        focusedFileIndex: null,
+        focusedFileLine: null,
+      },
+      chat: {
+        messages: [],
+        isStreaming: false,
+        streamingMessageId: null,
+        error: null,
+      },
+    },
+  });
+
+  return render(
+    <Provider store={store}>
+      <PRViewer />
+    </Provider>
+  );
+}
+
+describe('PRViewer signals tab', () => {
+  it('renders loading state', async () => {
+    const user = userEvent.setup();
+    renderViewer({ status: 'loading', data: null, error: null });
+    await user.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Signals' }));
+    expect(screen.getByText('Loading CI and scanning signals...')).toBeInTheDocument();
+  });
+
+  it('renders unavailable semantics without implying passing', async () => {
+    const user = userEvent.setup();
+    renderViewer({
+      status: 'success',
+      data: {
+        headSha: 'abcdef123456',
+        fetchedAt: Date.now(),
+        checks: { sourceState: 'unavailable', total: 0, failing: 0, pending: 0, items: [] },
+        statuses: { sourceState: 'error', state: null, statuses: [] },
+        scanning: {
+          sourceState: 'unavailable',
+          openAlerts: 0,
+          highSeverityCount: 0,
+          severityBuckets: {
+            critical: 0,
+            high: 0,
+            medium: 0,
+            low: 0,
+            error: 0,
+            warning: 0,
+            note: 0,
+            none: 0,
+            unknown: 0,
+          },
+          items: [],
+        },
+      },
+      error: null,
+    });
+
+    await user.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Signals' }));
+    expect(screen.getAllByText(/not confirmed passing/i).length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
+    expect(screen.queryByText(/all passing/i)).not.toBeInTheDocument();
+  });
+
+  it('renders failing check highlights in deterministic order', async () => {
+    const user = userEvent.setup();
+    const { container } = renderViewer({
+      status: 'success',
+      data: {
+        headSha: 'abcdef123456',
+        fetchedAt: Date.now(),
+        checks: {
+          sourceState: 'ok',
+          total: 3,
+          failing: 2,
+          pending: 0,
+          items: [
+            { id: 1, name: 'bbb', status: 'completed', conclusion: 'failure' },
+            { id: 2, name: 'aaa', status: 'completed', conclusion: 'action_required' },
+            { id: 3, name: 'ccc', status: 'completed', conclusion: 'success' },
+          ],
+        },
+        statuses: { sourceState: 'ok-empty', state: null, statuses: [] },
+        scanning: {
+          sourceState: 'ok-empty',
+          openAlerts: 0,
+          highSeverityCount: 0,
+          severityBuckets: {
+            critical: 0,
+            high: 0,
+            medium: 0,
+            low: 0,
+            error: 0,
+            warning: 0,
+            note: 0,
+            none: 0,
+            unknown: 0,
+          },
+          items: [],
+        },
+      },
+      error: null,
+    });
+
+    await user.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Signals' }));
+    const card = screen.getByText('Failing checks').closest('div');
+    expect(card).toBeTruthy();
+    if (card) {
+      const failingList = within(card).getByText('aaa (action_required)');
+      expect(failingList).toBeInTheDocument();
+    }
+
+    const text = container.textContent ?? '';
+    expect(text.indexOf('aaa (action_required)')).toBeLessThan(text.indexOf('bbb (failure)'));
+  });
+});

+          llmModel: 'gpt-4o',
+          demoMode: false,
+          summaryEnabled: true,
+          summaryPrompt: 'default summary prompt',
+          summaryCommands: '',
+        },
+      },
+      prs: {
+        selectedPR: selectedPRFixture,
+        activeRepository: null,
+        prList: [],
+        files: [],
+        comments: [],
+        reviewComments: [],
+        reviews: [],
+        commits: [],
+        summary: {
+          status: 'idle' as const,
+          content: null,
+          generatedAt: null,
+          error: null,
+          requestKey: null,
+          citations: [],
+          hasUncitedContent: false,
+        },
+        signals: {
+          ...signalsState,
+          requestKey: null,
+        },
+        isLoading: false,
+        error: null,
+        loadingByResource: {
+          metadata: false,
+          files: false,
+          comments: false,
+          reviewComments: false,
+          reviews: false,
+          commits: false,
+          prList: false,
+        },
+        errorByResource: {
+          metadata: null,
+          files: null,
+          comments: null,
+          reviewComments: null,
+          reviews: null,
+          commits: null,
+          prList: null,
+        },
+        latestRequestKeyByResource: {
+          metadata: null,
+          files: null,
+          comments: null,
+          reviewComments: null,
+          reviews: null,
+          commits: null,
+        },
+        focusedFileIndex: null,
+        focusedFileLine: null,
+      },
+      chat: {
+        messages: [],
+        isStreaming: false,
+        streamingMessageId: null,
+        error: null,
+      },
+    },
+  });
+
+  return render(
+    <Provider store={store}>
+      <PRViewer />
+    </Provider>
+  );
+}
+
+describe('PRViewer signals tab', () => {
+  it('renders loading state', async () => {
+    const user = userEvent.setup();
+    renderViewer({ status: 'loading', data: null, error: null });
+    await user.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Signals' }));
+    expect(screen.getByText('Loading CI and scanning signals...')).toBeInTheDocument();
+  });
+
+  it('renders unavailable semantics without implying passing', async () => {
+    const user = userEvent.setup();
+    renderViewer({
+      status: 'success',
+      data: {
+        headSha: 'abcdef123456',
+        fetchedAt: Date.now(),
+        checks: { sourceState: 'unavailable', total: 0, failing: 0, pending: 0, items: [] },
+        statuses: { sourceState: 'error', state: null, statuses: [] },
+        scanning: {
+          sourceState: 'unavailable',
+          openAlerts: 0,
+          highSeverityCount: 0,
+          severityBuckets: {
+            critical: 0,
+            high: 0,
+            medium: 0,
+            low: 0,
+            error: 0,
+            warning: 0,
+            note: 0,
+            none: 0,
+            unknown: 0,
+          },
+          items: [],
+        },
+      },
+      error: null,
+    });
+
+    await user.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Signals' }));
+    expect(screen.getAllByText(/not confirmed passing/i).length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
+    expect(screen.queryByText(/all passing/i)).not.toBeInTheDocument();
+  });
+
+  it('renders failing check highlights in deterministic order', async () => {
+    const user = userEvent.setup();
+    const { container } = renderViewer({
+      status: 'success',
+      data: {
+        headSha: 'abcdef123456',
+        fetchedAt: Date.now(),
+        checks: {
+          sourceState: 'ok',
+          total: 3,
+          failing: 2,
+          pending: 0,
+          items: [
+            { id: 1, name: 'bbb', status: 'completed', conclusion: 'failure' },
+            { id: 2, name: 'aaa', status: 'completed', conclusion: 'action_required' },
+            { id: 3, name: 'ccc', status: 'completed', conclusion: 'success' },
+          ],
+        },
+        statuses: { sourceState: 'ok-empty', state: null, statuses: [] },
+        scanning: {
+          sourceState: 'ok-empty',
+          openAlerts: 0,
+          highSeverityCount: 0,
+          severityBuckets: {
+            critical: 0,
+            high: 0,
+            medium: 0,
+            low: 0,
+            error: 0,
+            warning: 0,
+            note: 0,
+            none: 0,
+            unknown: 0,
+          },
+          items: [],
+        },
+      },
+      error: null,
+    });
+
+    await user.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Signals' }));
+    const card = screen.getByText('Failing checks').closest('div');
+    expect(card).toBeTruthy();
+    if (card) {
+      const failingList = within(card).getByText('aaa (action_required)');
+      expect(failingList).toBeInTheDocument();
+    }
+
+    const text = container.textContent ?? '';
+    expect(text.indexOf('aaa (action_required)')).toBeLessThan(text.indexOf('bbb (failure)'));
+  });
+});

parts.text:
Thanks — that symptom is consistent with a streaming parse gap, not model inactivity.

Most likely cause from current code:
- `chatStream()` only emits chunks from `choices[0].delta.content` in `src/services/llm.ts:115`.
- Some OpenAI-compatible backends/models stream text in alternate fields (for example reasoning deltas or array-style content parts), so GPU runs but UI receives zero appendable text.
- Summary still works because it uses non-stream `chat()` path, which reads `message.content` from the final response.

What I’d do next (no edits yet, just plan):
- Reproduce and inspect one chat stream payload in browser network to confirm actual delta shape.
- Update stream parsing to support common variants (string `content`, array content parts, and reasoning-style fields when no content exists).
- Add a safety fallback: if stream completes with zero visible text, do one non-stream `chat()` request and render that result.
- Add tests in `src/services/llm.test.ts` for these alternate SSE payloads and empty-stream fallback behavior.
- Add one integration test in `src/components/ChatWindow.test.tsx` ensuring assistant message is populated when stream chunks are non-standard.

If you want, I can execute that fix next.

One quick detail that helps me pick the best parser paths: which backend/model are you using in demo mode (e.g. LM Studio, Ollama, vLLM, LiteLLM + model name)?

Perfect, that aligns with the likely root cause.

`google/gemma-4-e4b` via LM Studio often uses OpenAI-compatible streaming with non-`delta.content` fields in some turns, so our parser in `src/services/llm.ts` can end up yielding nothing even though generation is happening.

**Plan**
- Update stream extraction in `src/services/llm.ts` to handle multiple delta shapes, not only `choices[0].delta.content`:
  - string content
  - array content parts (`[{ type: "output_text", text: "..." }]`)
  - reasoning-style fields (`reasoning_content` / `thinking`) as fallback when no regular content exists
- Add a “zero-visible-output” safeguard in `ChatWindow` flow (`src/components/ChatWindow.tsx`):
  - if stream completes with empty visible text, do one non-stream `chat()` call and render that result
  - keep current error behavior unchanged for true API failures
- Add unit tests in `src/services/llm.test.ts` for LM Studio/Gemma chunk variants (including no `delta.content` case).
- Add/extend chat integration tests in `src/components/ChatWindow.test.tsx` to verify user-visible assistant output for non-standard stream chunks.
- Optional usability tweak: if only reasoning text is emitted, prefix discreetly (e.g. “Model reasoning: …”) or treat it as normal assistant content.

One preference before I implement:  
1. **Recommended:** show reasoning tokens as normal assistant text when that’s all we get.  
2. Hide reasoning tokens and only show final answer text (if present).

Great — we’ll go with **show reasoning text as normal assistant output when that’s all the stream provides**.

Here’s the implementation plan (no changes made yet):

- Update streaming parser in `src/services/llm.ts` to extract text from multiple chunk shapes in priority order:
  - `choices[].delta.content` (string)
  - `choices[].delta.content` (array parts like `output_text` / `text`)
  - fallback reasoning fields (`reasoning_content`, `thinking`, etc.) when regular content is absent
- Keep output deterministic and safe:
  - normalize to plain string chunks
  - ignore empty/whitespace-only fragments
  - continue tolerating malformed JSON SSE lines (existing behavior)
- Add empty-visible-stream fallback in `src/components/ChatWindow.tsx`:
  - after stream completes, if accumulated visible output is empty, call `llmService.chat(...)` once with same message history
  - write fallback result into the same assistant message and finalize normally
  - preserve current abort/error handling behavior
- Ensure citations still work:
  - run `dispatchCitations(...)` on whichever final content path is used (stream or fallback)
- Add tests in `src/services/llm.test.ts`:
  - stream with non-standard LM Studio/Gemma chunk shape
  - stream with reasoning-only chunks
  - mixed chunks where normal content eventually appears
- Add tests in `src/components/ChatWindow.test.tsx`:
  - streaming yields no visible text but fallback `chat()` returns content -> assistant message is populated
  - reasoning-only stream still displays output
- Validate with:
  - `npm run lint -- --max-warnings=0`
  - `npm run test`
  - `npm run build`

If you want, next step I’ll execute this exact plan and report back with the concrete diff.

Index: openspec/changes/add-ci-and-code-scanning-signal-fusion/.openspec.yaml
===================================================================
--- openspec/changes/add-ci-and-code-scanning-signal-fusion/.openspec.yaml	
+++ openspec/changes/add-ci-and-code-scanning-signal-fusion/.openspec.yaml	
@@ -1,2 +0,0 @@
-schema: spec-driven
-created: 2026-03-20

Index: openspec/changes/add-ci-and-code-scanning-signal-fusion/design.md
===================================================================
--- openspec/changes/add-ci-and-code-scanning-signal-fusion/design.md	
+++ openspec/changes/add-ci-and-code-scanning-signal-fusion/design.md	
@@ -1,79 +0,0 @@
-## Context
-
-The application currently evaluates PRs from metadata, diffs, and discussion context, but it does not incorporate CI outcomes or security scanning signals that are already available in GitHub. Reviewers therefore lack an integrated view of execution and security posture while reading assistant guidance.
-
-This change spans API integration, state orchestration, prompt construction, and UI presentation, requiring a coordinated design.
-
-## Goals / Non-Goals
-
-**Goals:**
-- Fetch and normalize CI/check and code-scanning signals for the selected PR head SHA.
-- Surface signal health directly in the reviewer UI with clear pass/fail/pending/error states.
-- Inject normalized signals into summary/chat context so model output reflects real check and security state.
-
-**Non-Goals:**
-- This change does not own CI execution, reruns, or workflow management.
-- This change does not guarantee repository-wide security coverage beyond available API permissions.
-- This change does not implement organization-wide policy gating or enforcement.
-
-## Decisions
-
-### Decision: Build a normalized signal model in app state
-- **Choice:** Add a signal aggregation model in `prsSlice` with additive state (`status`, `checks`, `statuses`, `scanning`, `error`, `fetchedAt`).
-- **Rationale:** A normalized model isolates GitHub API heterogeneity and gives UI/LLM layers a stable contract.
-- **Alternatives considered:**
-  - Keep raw endpoint payloads in state: rejected because UI and prompts would be tightly coupled to API shape.
-
-### Decision: Fetch signals by PR head SHA via dedicated GitHub service methods
-- **Choice:** Add service methods for status/check-runs and code-scanning lookups tied to active PR head SHA.
-- **Rationale:** Head-SHA scoping prevents stale branch-level interpretation and aligns with reviewer expectations.
-- **Alternatives considered:**
-  - Fetch only PR-level metadata checks summary: rejected because details needed for actionable guidance are lost.
-
-### Decision: Treat unavailable or unauthorized signals as first-class fallback states
-- **Choice:** Explicitly represent unavailable/permission-limited signal states and render them distinctly from healthy/empty states.
-- **Rationale:** Silent omission would mislead users into assuming no risk signals exist.
-- **Alternatives considered:**
-  - Hide unavailable sources: rejected because it obscures confidence and data completeness.
-
-### Decision: Fuse signals into summary guidance with bounded verbosity
-- **Choice:** Prompt contract includes top failing/pending/high-severity signals and limits signal detail to reviewer-relevant highlights, with channel-specific depth (richer for summary, compact for chat).
-- **Rationale:** Improves prioritization without overwhelming summaries.
-- **Alternatives considered:**
-  - Dump full signal payload into prompt: rejected due to noise and token waste.
-
-### Decision: Preserve semantic separation of unavailable, error, and empty signal states
-- **Choice:** Represent source states distinctly in state, UI, and prompt context (`ok-empty`, `unavailable`, `error`) and prevent fallback collapse.
-- **Rationale:** Distinct semantics prevent false confidence and keep reviewer trust when visibility is incomplete.
-- **Alternatives considered:**
-  - Treat unavailable/error as no findings: rejected because it can mislead reviewers.
-
-### Decision: Enforce deterministic ranking and top-N caps for signal highlights
-- **Choice:** Rank signals by reviewer impact and include only bounded top-N items per signal category in prompt context.
-- **Rationale:** Keeps prompt size predictable, reduces noise, and stabilizes model behavior.
-- **Alternatives considered:**
-  - Include all signal items: rejected due to prompt bloat and degraded focus.
-
-## Risks / Trade-offs
-
-- [API permission variability across repos/orgs] -> Mitigation: add explicit per-source unavailable states and user-facing guidance.
-- [Increased API call volume] -> Mitigation: fetch once per PR head SHA selection and reuse existing refresh flows.
-- [Signal drift after new commits] -> Mitigation: invalidate signal data on head SHA change and re-fetch.
-- [Prompt bloat from signal details] -> Mitigation: include compact normalized summaries with strict cap on items.
-
-## Migration Plan
-
-1. Add signal endpoint methods and typed response normalization in `src/services/github.ts` and related types.
-2. Extend `prsSlice` with signal lifecycle state and loading/error handling.
-3. Add signal fetch orchestration on PR load/refresh and head SHA changes.
-4. Add Signals UI rendering with explicit unavailable/error/empty/success states.
-5. Inject normalized signals into summary/chat prompt context and validate output quality.
-6. Add tests for service mapping, state transitions, and UI rendering paths.
-
-Rollback strategy: gate signal fetching/rendering/prompt fusion behind a feature toggle; if disabled/removed, app returns to existing diff-and-discussion-only behavior while preserving explicit unavailable/error semantics when signal UI is shown.
-
-## Open Questions
-
-- Which specific code-scanning endpoints should be default-on vs optional due to permission cost?
-- Should signal summaries include historical trend (last green) or only current state?
-- Do we need per-repo setting to disable code-scanning fetches for noisy environments?

Index: openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-14-add-ci-and-code-scanning-signal-fusion/design.md
===================================================================
--- openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-14-add-ci-and-code-scanning-signal-fusion/design.md	
+++ openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-14-add-ci-and-code-scanning-signal-fusion/design.md	
@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+## Context
+
+The application currently evaluates PRs from metadata, diffs, and discussion context, but it does not incorporate CI outcomes or security scanning signals that are already available in GitHub. Reviewers therefore lack an integrated view of execution and security posture while reading assistant guidance.
+
+This change spans API integration, state orchestration, prompt construction, and UI presentation, requiring a coordinated design.
+
+## Goals / Non-Goals
+
+**Goals:**
+- Fetch and normalize CI/check and code-scanning signals for the selected PR head SHA.
+- Surface signal health directly in the reviewer UI with clear pass/fail/pending/error states.
+- Inject normalized signals into summary/chat context so model output reflects real check and security state.
+
+**Non-Goals:**
+- This change does not own CI execution, reruns, or workflow management.
+- This change does not guarantee repository-wide security coverage beyond available API permissions.
+- This change does not implement organization-wide policy gating or enforcement.
+
+## Decisions
+
+### Decision: Build a normalized signal model in app state
+- **Choice:** Add a signal aggregation model in `prsSlice` with additive state (`status`, `checks`, `statuses`, `scanning`, `error`, `fetchedAt`).
+- **Rationale:** A normalized model isolates GitHub API heterogeneity and gives UI/LLM layers a stable contract.
+- **Alternatives considered:**
+  - Keep raw endpoint payloads in state: rejected because UI and prompts would be tightly coupled to API shape.
+
+### Decision: Fetch signals by PR head SHA via dedicated GitHub service methods
+- **Choice:** Add service methods for status/check-runs and code-scanning lookups tied to active PR head SHA.
+- **Rationale:** Head-SHA scoping prevents stale branch-level interpretation and aligns with reviewer expectations.
+- **Alternatives considered:**
+  - Fetch only PR-level metadata checks summary: rejected because details needed for actionable guidance are lost.
+
+### Decision: Treat unavailable or unauthorized signals as first-class fallback states
+- **Choice:** Explicitly represent unavailable/permission-limited signal states and render them distinctly from healthy/empty states.
+- **Rationale:** Silent omission would mislead users into assuming no risk signals exist.
+- **Alternatives considered:**
+  - Hide unavailable sources: rejected because it obscures confidence and data completeness.
+
+### Decision: Fuse signals into summary guidance with bounded verbosity
+- **Choice:** Prompt contract includes top failing/pending/high-severity signals and limits signal detail to reviewer-relevant highlights, with channel-specific depth (richer for summary, compact for chat).
+- **Rationale:** Improves prioritization without overwhelming summaries.
+- **Alternatives considered:**
+  - Dump full signal payload into prompt: rejected due to noise and token waste.
+
+### Decision: Preserve semantic separation of unavailable, error, and empty signal states
+- **Choice:** Represent source states distinctly in state, UI, and prompt context (`ok-empty`, `unavailable`, `error`) and prevent fallback collapse.
+- **Rationale:** Distinct semantics prevent false confidence and keep reviewer trust when visibility is incomplete.
+- **Alternatives considered:**
+  - Treat unavailable/error as no findings: rejected because it can mislead reviewers.
+
+### Decision: Enforce deterministic ranking and top-N caps for signal highlights
+- **Choice:** Rank signals by reviewer impact and include only bounded top-N items per signal category in prompt context.
+- **Rationale:** Keeps prompt size predictable, reduces noise, and stabilizes model behavior.
+- **Alternatives considered:**
+  - Include all signal items: rejected due to prompt bloat and degraded focus.
+
+## Risks / Trade-offs
+
+- [API permission variability across repos/orgs] -> Mitigation: add explicit per-source unavailable states and user-facing guidance.
+- [Increased API call volume] -> Mitigation: fetch once per PR head SHA selection and reuse existing refresh flows.
+- [Signal drift after new commits] -> Mitigation: invalidate signal data on head SHA change and re-fetch.
+- [Prompt bloat from signal details] -> Mitigation: include compact normalized summaries with strict cap on items.
+
+## Migration Plan
+
+1. Add signal endpoint methods and typed response normalization in `src/services/github.ts` and related types.
+2. Extend `prsSlice` with signal lifecycle state and loading/error handling.
+3. Add signal fetch orchestration on PR load/refresh and head SHA changes.
+4. Add Signals UI rendering with explicit unavailable/error/empty/success states.
+5. Inject normalized signals into summary/chat prompt context and validate output quality.
+6. Add tests for service mapping, state transitions, and UI rendering paths.
+
+Rollback strategy: gate signal fetching/rendering/prompt fusion behind a feature toggle; if disabled/removed, app returns to existing diff-and-discussion-only behavior while preserving explicit unavailable/error semantics when signal UI is shown.
+
+## Open Questions
+
+- Which specific code-scanning endpoints should be default-on vs optional due to permission cost?
+- Should signal summaries include historical trend (last green) or only current state?
+- Do we need per-repo setting to disable code-scanning fetches for noisy environments?

+## Why
+
+Current review analysis relies mostly on PR metadata, comments, and diffs, which can miss high-signal runtime and security context already available in GitHub checks and scanning systems. Bringing CI and code-scanning signals into the review loop improves prioritization and helps reviewers focus on real risk faster.
+
+## What Changes
+
+- Add signal fusion that fetches CI/check status data and relevant code-scanning indicators for the selected PR head SHA.
+- Expose a reviewer-facing Signals view and status summaries that clearly identify failing/pending/high-risk items.
+- Enrich summary generation and chat context with normalized signal data so assistant guidance reflects CI/security reality.
+
+## Capabilities
+
+### New Capabilities
+- `pr-signal-fusion`: Aggregation, normalization, and presentation of CI/check/code-scanning signals for selected PRs, including channel-aware fusion into summary/chat assistant context.
+
+### Modified Capabilities
+- `pr-review-summary`: Summary output contract is extended to incorporate high-risk CI/scanning signals into reviewer guidance when available.
+
+## Impact
+
+- Affected code: `src/services/github.ts`, `src/store/slices/prsSlice.ts`, `src/services/llm.ts`, `src/components/PRViewer.tsx`, and related tests/types.
+- APIs: additional GitHub REST endpoints for statuses/check-runs and security scanning metadata.
+- Dependencies/systems: no required external services beyond existing GitHub integration; feature depends on repository permissions and token scopes.
